title: Process for a Conference Sweepstakes (w/wo a Survey)
content:
Process for a Conference Sweepstakes (w/wo a Survey)

This process is for use by the Marketing Teams to conduct sweepstakes (with or without a survey), at a GitLab booth at a non-GitLab sponsored conference.

This process uses an automated workflow to generate rules. This is accomplished through a Google Form and not through a GitLab issue. Provided that the request satisfies the eligibility requirements , the requestor will receive a set of abbreviated rules almost immediately (minutes to an hour). If the request does not satisfy the pre-determined requirements, use of the Promotional Games Legal Template will be required. Team members should also be familiar with GitLab’s Gifts and Entertainment Policy , particularly, restrictions that may apply to government official participation.

Review the Eligibility Requirements for proceeding under this Process . Sweepstakes which do not meet the Eligibility Requirements in full cannot proceed under this process. Refer to the mainstream review and approval process for engaging legal for approval of a proposed promotional game.

Complete the Request Rules for a Conference Sweepstakes Google form.

You will receive an email with the abbreviated rules as an attachment. Please review the Standard Promotional Rules and Abbreviated rules section for details on how the abbreviated rules work in connection with the standard set of rules created for this process.

If conducting a survey, create a separate screen/page within the survey where the participant can select to be entered into the sweepstakes and view the abbreviated rules. Follow the guidelines for the proper administration of the sweepstakes below when creating this screen/page.

Determine eligibility of all entries prior to the selection of the winner(s) as described in the administrative responsibilities .

Validate and verify the proposed winner(s) prior to distributing prizes. Instructions for this are located in the administrative responsibilities section below.

Eligibility Requirements for Proceeding Under this Process

The only type of promotional game permitted under this process is a sweepstakes. Please see this section of the handbook to learn about the types of promotional games permitted at GitLab.

Entry must be via one of the following methods: A QR code located at GitLab’s booth. Entrants must scan the QR code to be brought to the entry form. Visit the URL identified in the abbreviated rules, and complete the entry form. Complete the survey at the URL identified in the abbreviated rules. The participant will be given the opportunity to provide their information including their email address at the end of the survey in order to be entered for a chance to win a prize.

A QR code located at GitLab’s booth. Entrants must scan the QR code to be brought to the entry form.

Visit the URL identified in the abbreviated rules, and complete the entry form.

Complete the survey at the URL identified in the abbreviated rules. The participant will be given the opportunity to provide their information including their email address at the end of the survey in order to be entered for a chance to win a prize.

The value of each individual prize must be less than $600 USD. See #5.9. for an exceptions.

The total aggregate value of all prizes must be less than $1,000 USD. See #5.10. and #5.11. for exceptions.

Eligible countries where a sweepstakes can be conducted: Austria Canada (excluding Quebec) Ireland Japan Switzerland United Kingdom United States France and Germany can be included provided the rules and all promotional materials are translated into French and German. Legal does not provide translation services. Denmark can be included provided the value of each individual prize is less than $30 USD. New Zealand can be included provided the total aggregate value of all prizes is less than $500 USD. The Netherlands can be included provided: The total aggregate value of all prizes is less than $500 USD; Advertisements include the full Abbreviated Rules. Australia can be included provided: Winners are notified within 2 days of the drawing; Records are kept of entries and winners for 12 months after the promotion ends; Any prizes shown in advertisements are the actual prizes awarded; Prizes are not substituted.

Canada (excluding Quebec)

France and Germany can be included provided the rules and all promotional materials are translated into French and German. Legal does not provide translation services.

Denmark can be included provided the value of each individual prize is less than $30 USD.

New Zealand can be included provided the total aggregate value of all prizes is less than $500 USD.

The Netherlands can be included provided: The total aggregate value of all prizes is less than $500 USD; Advertisements include the full Abbreviated Rules.

The total aggregate value of all prizes is less than $500 USD;

Advertisements include the full Abbreviated Rules.

Australia can be included provided: Winners are notified within 2 days of the drawing; Records are kept of entries and winners for 12 months after the promotion ends; Any prizes shown in advertisements are the actual prizes awarded; Prizes are not substituted.

Winners are notified within 2 days of the drawing;

Records are kept of entries and winners for 12 months after the promotion ends;

Any prizes shown in advertisements are the actual prizes awarded;

Prizes are not substituted.

Prohibited Countries where a sweepstakes cannot be conducted: Not allowed due to gambling laws: South Korea Indonesia Belgium License, permit, or registration required: Philippines Thailand (also, winner selection by a computer or other automated means is forbidden) India UAE Israel (notification to the Ministry of Finance required) Sweden Spain Saudi Arabia Tax withholding requirements: Taiwan Singapore Embargoed Countries: North Korea Cuba Iran Syria Russia

Not allowed due to gambling laws: South Korea Indonesia Belgium

License, permit, or registration required: Philippines Thailand (also, winner selection by a computer or other automated means is forbidden) India UAE Israel (notification to the Ministry of Finance required) Sweden Spain Saudi Arabia

Thailand (also, winner selection by a computer or other automated means is forbidden)

Israel (notification to the Ministry of Finance required)

Tax withholding requirements: Taiwan Singapore

Embargoed Countries: North Korea Cuba Iran Syria Russia

Participants in the sweepstakes must: Be at the age of majority in their locale on the date of entry; Be physically present at the conference during the Promotion Period; Not be a national from any of the Embargoed Countries; Not be an exhibitor at the conference; Not be an employee of GitLab; and Not be a family member or living in the same household as a GitLab team member.

Be at the age of majority in their locale on the date of entry;

Be physically present at the conference during the Promotion Period;

Not be a national from any of the Embargoed Countries;

Not be an exhibitor at the conference;

Not be an employee of GitLab; and

Not be a family member or living in the same household as a GitLab team member.

Proper Administration of the sweepstakes

Entry Forms: All entry forms must include the abbreviated rules with a link to the full official rules. Surveys: Within your survey you will need to add a separate screen/page where the participant can select to be entered into the sweepstakes. A suggested way to do this is to have the participant tick a box yes/no to enter the sweepstakes, and if they tick “yes” then capture the required information (no boxes should be pre-ticked). To enter, collect the name, email, country, and zip/postal code. If someone attempts to enter with a country that is not within the promotional territory, the system collecting entries should automatically flag that entry as ineligible. You are not required to notify the entrant that they are ineligible. On the screen/page where the sweepstakes is discussed, certain information must appear prior to any tick box or text field to enter. The participant must be presented with the abbreviated rules (not a link to the abbreviated rules) after a one sentence summary inviting them to enter. By way of example, it can read as follows:

All entry forms must include the abbreviated rules with a link to the full official rules.

Surveys: Within your survey you will need to add a separate screen/page where the participant can select to be entered into the sweepstakes. A suggested way to do this is to have the participant tick a box yes/no to enter the sweepstakes, and if they tick “yes” then capture the required information (no boxes should be pre-ticked). To enter, collect the name, email, country, and zip/postal code. If someone attempts to enter with a country that is not within the promotional territory, the system collecting entries should automatically flag that entry as ineligible. You are not required to notify the entrant that they are ineligible. On the screen/page where the sweepstakes is discussed, certain information must appear prior to any tick box or text field to enter. The participant must be presented with the abbreviated rules (not a link to the abbreviated rules) after a one sentence summary inviting them to enter. By way of example, it can read as follows:

On the screen/page where the sweepstakes is discussed, certain information must appear prior to any tick box or text field to enter. The participant must be presented with the abbreviated rules (not a link to the abbreviated rules) after a one sentence summary inviting them to enter. By way of example, it can read as follows:

You may advertise the sweepstakes via email distribution to those attending the conference or in-person at the conference, before or during the event, provided that all advertising includes the abbreviated rules with a link to the full official rules. The abbreviated rules should be part of the same visual space as the remainder of the advertising message. Advertising should not be made available after the sweepstakes has ended, or to any person not attending the conference.

No changes are permitted to the sweepstakes, the abbreviated rules, or the plan for advertising after the time period to enter has begun. The reason for this is that the countries designated as eligible under this process were selected in part on the basis that sweepstakes are allowed, and for some countries, under specific conditions. If you wish to advertise to additional persons or a broader audience to raise interest in the survey using the sweepstakes as an incentive, then you should not use this process and instead use the regular channel of seeking legal approval for a promotional game .

Sweepstakes Call-Out Language: Expressing Chance

Marketing materials that reference a sweepstakes must communicate that winning is based on chance. This is required for every call-out, headline or promotional statement connected to the sweepstakes. The language should not imply that the entrant will win, rather than might win. If the language can be read as a promise or guarantee of a prize, revise it to include a chance qualifier such as, “chance”, “could” or “might.”

The following expresses chance, and can be used: “Enter for a chance to win…” “You could win…” “You might win…”

“Enter for a chance to win…”

The following implies guaranteed winning, and should not be used without a chance qualifier: “Win a…” “Get a…” “Receive a…”

When in doubt, ask yourself if the language suggests that everyone who enters will win something, or, that a winner is selected by chance. If it’s the former, rewrite it. If you have any questions, reach out to @LeeFalc .

Administrative Responsibilities

The DRI for each sweepstakes is responsible for determining the eligibility of entries prior to selecting a winner. This may be done manually or through an automated process and should include the rejection of all entries by an individual who submits more than 1 entry, and the rejection of any entries which do not meet eligibility requirements.

The DRI for each sweepstakes is also responsible for qualifying every proposed winner prior to distributing prizes. This is accomplished through the use of a Declaration of Eligibility and Release which should be emailed to the proposed winner when they are initially notified. There is a defined window of time for the Declaration to be returned by the proposed winner and failure to do so will result in forfeiture of the Prize. Should a proposed Winner fail to return the Declaration, the DRI may select an alternate winner from the remaining pool of eligible entries, but you are not required to do so. The Declaration of Eligibility and Release will contain personal data of the winner and therefore must be treated in accordance with GitLab standards for the security and protection of personal data. It must also be retained for 24 months after the winner is confirmed. Google Drive folder to store the full executed Declarations. Please create a separate folder for each sweepstakes.

Google Drive folder to store the full executed Declarations. Please create a separate folder for each sweepstakes.

If the prize will be shipped to the winner(s) rather than distributed at the conference, the DRI will need to confirm with GitLab’s vendor, Brilliant, that they will ship to the winner’s country, as Brilliant does not ship to all countries/territories.

Marketing Materials Opt-in Language

Express consent is required before sending marketing materials to those who enter a sweepstakes and/or take a survey. The following opt-in language, with a check box, will need to be included at the top of the entry form:

I consent to receive marketing communications from GitLab about its products, services, and events and understand that I can opt-out at any time by unsubscribing in emails or visiting GitLab’s communication preference center .

Standard Promotional Rules and Abbreviated Rules

There is a set of official English language rules which applies to every sweepstakes offered and is permanently hosted here . A German language version of the official rules is permanently hosted here . The official rules are structured and formatted so that they apply to every sweepstakes.  The details which apply to a specific sweepstakes are outlined in the abbreviated rules - generated through the automated workflow of the Google form.

If any changes are needed to the abbreviated rules before the sweepstakes begins and before any advertising has been launched, reach out to @LeeFalc .

Abbreviated rules will generate upon submission of the Google Form and will look similar to this:
